programs.title: Responsible AI Program
programs.summary: Four-week Responsible AI learning arc for foundations, ethics, societal impact, human agency, and capstone practice.

modules.title: Foundations
modules.objective: Explain what AI systems are, where they show up, and how to evaluate their limits.

modules.title: Ethics And Impact
modules.objective: Connect generated examples to bias, privacy, transparency, and community impact decisions.

modules.title: Capstone Practice
modules.objective: Use a visual artifact to defend a responsible AI decision and identify the next human checkpoint.

programs.title: Upgrade AI
programs.summary: Marketing, newsletter, podcast, and training visuals that turn AI practice into teachable public formats.

modules.title: Practical Prompting
modules.objective: Translate a work goal into useful prompt inputs, constraints, and review criteria.

modules.title: Publishing System
modules.objective: Turn generated assets into a repeatable newsletter, podcast, or campaign package.

programs.title: HOPECODE
programs.summary: Knowledge gardens, power maps, relationship maps, values, skill trees, and anti-corporate systems thinking.

modules.title: Knowledge Garden
modules.objective: Map concepts, people, values, and artifacts as a living learning system.

modules.title: Power And Relationship Maps
modules.objective: Use visual mapping to reason about influence, trust, dependency, and care.

programs.title: BC AI Ecosystem
programs.summary: Community, subgroup, event, and ecosystem diagrams for explaining the regional AI field.

modules.title: Community Map
modules.objective: Identify communities, subgroups, and stakeholder relationships in the BC AI ecosystem.

modules.title: Event Story
modules.objective: Turn event visuals into a coherent learning or public recap sequence.
